title: ADHD Natural Treatments With Peer-Reviewed Evidence
title_es: Tratamientos naturales para el TDAH con evidencia revisada por pares
description: Stimulant alternatives for ADHD are everywhere — saffron, omega-3, mindfulness, exercise, neurofeedback. Most are oversold. Some have surprisingly strong RCTs. We rank the 14 most-cited interventions by replication strength, effect size, and how they compare to methylphenidate head-to-head.
description_es: Las alternativas a los estimulantes para el TDAH están en todos lados — azafrán, omega-3, mindfulness, ejercicio, neurofeedback. La mayoría están sobrevendidas. Algunas tienen ensayos clínicos sorprendentemente sólidos. Clasificamos las 14 intervenciones más citadas por fuerza de replicación, tamaño del efecto y comparación con metilfenidato cara a cara.
author: Alejandro del Palacio
imageAlt: Vintage 1980s pediatric research clinic shelf — saffron threads in a glass Petri dish beside a vial of methylphenidate, both lit by a single amber lamp on a dark cobalt background
imagePrompt: Retrofuturist 1980s cinematic still photograph. Dim pediatric neurology research clinic dominates 70 percent of the frame in deep cobalt-black with heavy 35mm grain. Center frame: a polished steel laboratory shelf at chest height. On the shelf, arranged in clinical sequence from left to right: a clear glass Petri dish containing crimson-red saffron threads (Crocus sativus stigmas) in saturated electric scarlet glow, an amber prescription vial containing white methylphenidate tablets with a hand-typed prescription label, a small tin of dried omega-3 fish-oil capsules in golden translucent sheen, an EEG electrode cap on a foam mannequin head with multicolored leads in electric blue, and a small printed card listing INCA elimination diet foods (lamb, rice, pear, water). Behind the shelf, a freestanding chalkboard mounted on wheels carries a hand-drawn ranking diagram — fourteen numbered intervention boxes connected by arrows, with sample sizes and effect sizes (Cohen's d values) annotated in chalk. A single overhead surgical lamp casts an amber pool of light onto the shelf; the rest of the room recedes into deep cobalt-black shadow. Faint blue LED indicators glow on a vintage analog rating-scale machine in the foreground. The shelf items + amber lamp + electric blue chalk markers are the ONLY chromatic zones — vibrantly breaking out of the cobalt matrix. Heavy cinematic 35mm film grain, anamorphic lens flare, kodak ektachrome retro slide aesthetic. Mood: Tarkovsky Stalker + Soderbergh Solaris 2002 + early Cronenberg + Charles Bradley's 1937 Emma Pendleton Bradley Home pediatric ward. The dark room is the matrix; the ranked clinical sequence on the shelf is what actually moves the needle. No recognizable face. Mysterious pediatric-neurology forensic. Photographic NOT painterly NOT illustrated.
researchSources: Baziar et al. 2019 — saffron vs methylphenidate ADHD RCT
Cortese et al. 2015 — neurofeedback ADHD meta-analysis
Hoogman et al. 2017 — ENIGMA ADHD brain MRI
Mitchell et al. 2017 — mindfulness ADHD review
Pelsser et al. 2011 — restricted elimination diet INCA trial
seoTitle: ADHD Natural Treatment: 14 Interventions Ranked by RCT Evidence (2026)
seoTitle_es: Tratamiento natural del TDAH: 14 intervenciones clasificadas por evidencia RCT (2026)
seoDescription: Of 14 most-cited natural ADHD interventions, only 4 have head-to-head RCT evidence. Saffron matched methylphenidate (Baziar 2019). What works, what doesn't, dose, timing.
seoDescription_es: De 14 intervenciones naturales más citadas para el TDAH, solo 4 tienen evidencia RCT directa. El azafrán igualó al metilfenidato (Baziar 2019). Qué funciona, qué no, dosis, momento.
bluf: Only 3 natural ADHD interventions have RCT effect sizes comparable to stimulants: saffron (multiple Iranian RCTs, d≈0.7), high-EPA omega-3 (Cooper meta-analysis 2015, d=0.38), and aerobic exercise (Cerrillo-Urbina meta-analysis 2015, d=0.84). Everything else — neurofeedback, mindfulness, elimination diets — has weaker or contradictory evidence. The reality is messier than wellness lists admit.
bluf_es: Solo 3 intervenciones naturales para TDAH tienen tamaños de efecto en RCTs comparables a los estimulantes: azafrán (múltiples RCTs iraníes, d≈0.7), omega-3 alto en EPA (meta-análisis Cooper 2015, d=0.38) y ejercicio aeróbico (meta-análisis Cerrillo-Urbina 2015, d=0.84). Todo lo demás — neurofeedback, mindfulness, dietas de eliminación — tiene evidencia más débil o contradictoria. La realidad es más complicada de lo que admiten las listas de wellness.
